competency_groups.title: The Living World: Classification and Taxonomy
competency_groups.description: Master the fundamental principles of biodiversity, taxonomic hierarchy, and binomial nomenclature.
competencies: Explain the need for classification and describe the three domains of life.

competency_groups.title: Biological Classification: Five Kingdoms and Microbes
competency_groups.description: Analyze the five-kingdom system and identify characteristics of Monera, Protista, Fungi, Lichens, Viruses, and Viroids.
competencies: Classify organisms into kingdoms based on salient features.

competency_groups.title: Plant Kingdom: Major Groups and Features
competency_groups.description: Differentiate between major plant groups including Algae, Bryophyta, Pteridophyta, Gymnospermae, and Angiosperms.
competencies: Distinguish the salient features of all major plant divisions.

competency_groups.title: Animal Kingdom: Phyla and Classes
competency_groups.description: Classify animals based on body organization, distinguishing between non-chordates (up to phylum) and chordates (up to class).
competencies: Identify key characteristics of animal groups from invertebrates to vertebrates.

competency_groups.title: Morphology of Flowering Plants: Structural Adaptations
competency_groups.description: Describe the morphology and modifications of root, stem, leaf, inflorescence, flower, fruit, and seed.
competencies: Analyze morphological structures to identify plant adaptations.
competency_groups.unit_name: Structural Organization in Plants and Animals
